title: Casualty collection point layout
category: Medical and first · Aid · Disaster · Health
summary: Medical-heavy survival entry for safe entrance, triage table, walking wounded area, urgent area, quiet area, and exit route. It is designed for offline emergencies and does not replace professional care.
content: Use this entry to organize safe entrance, triage table, walking wounded area, urgent area, quiet area, and exit route. Start with scene safety, hand hygiene, calm communication, and written timing. Keep the person comfortable, protect privacy, avoid risky experiments, and prepare a clear handover for a doctor, ambulance, clinic, pharmacist, or trained first-aider. Watch especially for: crowding, blocked exits, mixed clean and dirty zones, no recorder, no water, or no referral route. In a long emergency, review the person regularly instead of assuming they are stable.
materials: clean water if available. Context: “Casualty collection point layout”.
soap or sanitizer if available — for «Casualty collection point layout»
gloves or clean barrier if available — for «Casualty collection point layout»
paper and pen. Context: “Casualty collection point layout”.
phone or radio if available — for «Casualty collection point layout»
labels for times and medicines — for «Casualty collection point layout»
steps: Check that the place is safe for the helper and the injured or sick person. Apply it specifically within the “Casualty collection point layout” procedure.
Call emergency help or send a reliable person for help when any red flag is present. Apply it specifically within the “Casualty collection point layout” procedure.
Write the time the problem started, what changed, medicines taken, allergies, and known conditions. Apply it specifically within the “Casualty collection point layout” procedure.
Use clean hands and clean barriers before touching wounds, body fluids, food, medicines, infants, or medical devices. Apply it specifically within the “Casualty collection point layout” procedure.
Keep the person warm or cool as appropriate, still if trauma is possible, and protected from crowds. Apply it specifically within the “Casualty collection point layout” procedure.
Recheck and record changes regularly so deterioration is noticed early. Apply it specifically within the “Casualty collection point layout” procedure.
Prepare a short handover note with name, age, symptoms, times, medicines, allergies, and actions already taken. Apply it specifically within the “Casualty collection point layout” procedure.
warnings: Do not use this as a replacement for training, emergency services, a clinician, or local medical guidance. Treat this as a specific boundary while managing “Casualty collection point layout”.

Do not guess medicine doses, mix medicines without label checking, share prescriptions, or perform invasive procedures. Treat this as a specific boundary while managing “Casualty collection point layout”.
Escalate urgently if you see: crowding, blocked exits, mixed clean and dirty zones, no recorder, no water, or no referral route.
Keep children, elders, pregnancy, disability, immune weakness, and chronic illness in the high-risk group. Treat this as a specific boundary while managing “Casualty collection point layout”.
common_mistakes: Waiting too long after red flags appear while carrying out “Casualty collection point layout”
Not writing times and medicines while carrying out “Casualty collection point layout”
Touching wounds or fluids without hand hygiene while carrying out “Casualty collection point layout”
Letting crowds block air, privacy, or transport while carrying out “Casualty collection point layout”
Assuming a quiet person is stable without rechecking while carrying out “Casualty collection point layout”
alternatives: Use official first-aid training when available as a fallback for “Casualty collection point layout”
Use local emergency numbers and pharmacists for medicine questions as a fallback for “Casualty collection point layout”
Use paper handover notes when phones are dead as a fallback for “Casualty collection point layout”
Use a trusted translator if language blocks care as a fallback for “Casualty collection point layout”
failure_signs: Red flags appear: crowding, blocked exits, mixed clean and dirty zones, no recorder, no water, or no referral route
The person becomes harder to wake, breathe, drink, speak, walk, or recognize during “Casualty collection point layout”
Pain, bleeding, swelling, fever, confusion, or weakness gets worse during “Casualty collection point layout”
when_not_to_use: Do not delay emergency care to complete the checklist when using the “Casualty collection point layout” record
Do not move a trauma patient unless staying is more dangerous when using the “Casualty collection point layout” record
Do not force care on a conscious person except immediate life safety situations under local rules when using the “Casualty collection point layout” record
short_term: Stabilize the scene, reduce immediate harm, record key facts, and connect the person to trained help. This note applies specifically to Casualty collection point layout.
long_term: Keep medical records updated, train the group in certified first aid, maintain supplies, and practice respectful handovers. This note applies specifically to Casualty collection point layout.
if_method_fails: Escalate to emergency services, nearest clinic, pharmacist, radio medical consult, or evacuation route; document the failed step and time. This note applies specifically to Casualty collection point layout.
environment_notes: In apartments, heat, smoke, poor ventilation, crowds, no elevator, and water outages can make medical problems worse. Plan quiet space, clean water, light, and transport. This note applies specifically to Casualty collection point layout.

Recheck and record changes regularly so deterioration is noticed early. Apply it specifically within the “Medical decision log for scarce care” procedure.
Prepare a short handover note with name, age, symptoms, times, medicines, allergies, and actions already taken. Apply it specifically within the “Medical decision log for scarce care” procedure.
warnings: Do not use this as a replacement for training, emergency services, a clinician, or local medical guidance. Treat this as a specific boundary while managing “Medical decision log for scarce care”.

Do not guess medicine doses, mix medicines without label checking, share prescriptions, or perform invasive procedures. Treat this as a specific boundary while managing “Medical decision log for scarce care”.
Escalate urgently if you see: favoritism claim, missing recheck, unclear priority, no witness, or supplies locked by one person.
Keep children, elders, pregnancy, disability, immune weakness, and chronic illness in the high-risk group. Treat this as a specific boundary while managing “Medical decision log for scarce care”.

Not writing times and medicines while carrying out “Medical decision log for scarce care”
Touching wounds or fluids without hand hygiene while carrying out “Medical decision log for scarce care”
Letting crowds block air, privacy, or transport while carrying out “Medical decision log for scarce care”
Assuming a quiet person is stable without rechecking while carrying out “Medical decision log for scarce care”

Use paper handover notes when phones are dead as a fallback for “Medical decision log for scarce care”
